name: Dialog
description: Focused, opt-in interruption — a centred card over a scrim that asks the user to make a single decision before continuing. Reach for Dialog when the surface needs an explicit commit / dismiss pair (onboarding nudge, confirm-before-destructive) and the answer can fit in a short title + one supporting paragraph. Anything longer than two action buttons or a paragraph of supporting copy belongs in a full screen or sheet, not a dialog.

image.description: { src, alt } — illustrative image between the title and the actions.

imageFirst.description: When true (default), the image sits directly under the title; when false, under the body.

primaryAction.description: { label, onClick } — delegates to Button appearance='primary', size='large', fullWidth.

secondaryAction.description: { label, onClick } — delegates to Button appearance='tertiary', size='large', fullWidth.

inline.description: When true, scopes the scrim and card to the nearest positioned ancestor instead of portaling to document.body.

scrim.description: Translucent black overlay covering the host surface; centres the container with flex. Clicking fires onClose. Holds the card to a 40px inset from the wrapper's left and right edges.

container.description: The dialog card. surfaceContainerHigh fill, radius.xl, elevation.overlay. Fills the available width up to max-width: 480px.

title.description: Short headline. heading.sm / Semibold / onSurface. Always sits at the top of the stack.

image.description: Single illustrative image between title and actions. Spans the card's inner width and preserves intrinsic aspect ratio.

body.description: One-paragraph supporting copy. body.sm / Regular / onSurfaceVariant.

actions.description: Vertical stack of action buttons at the bottom of the card. Primary on top, secondary below. Both stretch to inner width.

withImage.note: The image's visual mass anchors the composition; centred copy reads as a unified illustrated card.

textOnly.note: Left alignment matches the reading rhythm of the body copy.

alignment.actions: Always stretched to the card's inner width regardless of alignment.

open.description: Scrim + card both render; underlying surface remains in DOM but pointer events suppressed by the scrim's full-viewport overlay.

closed.description: Renders nothing — no reserved whitespace, no hidden DOM.

focusIndicator.description: The dialog itself isn't a focus target. Its action slots (primary / secondary Buttons) inherit Button's outward focus composition; on open, focus is moved to the primary action. See the contained sub-components for the visual contract.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

behavior.scrimClickCloses: Tapping anywhere on the scrim fires onClose. The card stops click propagation so a tap inside never closes the dialog by accident.
behavior.escapeKeyCloses: Pressing Esc fires onClose. Bound at the document level so the dialog doesn't need to own focus to receive the key.
behavior.focusManagement: On open, focus moves to the primary action so Enter commits. Focus returns to the previously-focused element on close (consumer-managed via onClose).
behavior.portalRendering: By default renders into a portal at document.body. Pass inline to scope to the nearest positioned ancestor; that ancestor must declare position: relative and overflow: hidden.
